acceptanceSignals: Order state transitions enforce cutoff and validation rules with explicit domain errors.
Inventory reservation/release is idempotent and concurrency-safe.
Order timeline includes modification, cancel, sold-out, and refund-related states.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Order State and Inventory Engine in 0 attempts

lastCommit.message: feat: implement Order State and Inventory Engine (attempt 5)

reinforcementResults.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The feature’s externally visible HTTP changes (new pickup-verification route plus order/timeline schema updates) are synchronized in the same change set across runtime route catalog, OpenAPI (`contract/openapi/openapi.yaml`/`.json`), and generated TypeScript client artifacts (`contract/generated/ts-client/*`). Contract conformance coverage is present for route-to-spec parity and error-response schema references, and the drift gate (`pnpm run contract:check-drift`) passes in this workspace.

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: The workspace satisfies the feature and active policy: strict order lifecycle transitions with explicit domain errors, fixed Asia/Taipei cutoff enforcement, atomic/idempotent inventory reservation-release with concurrency protection, timeline coverage for modified/cancelled/sold-out/refund states, and hard prelaunch throughput/latency SLO gate artifacts with no retained legacy/fallback execution paths.

reinforcementResults.name: Peak Load SLO Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The current workspace satisfies the active policy for this feature: strict order lifecycle and cutoff enforcement are implemented with explicit domain errors, inventory reservation/release behavior is idempotent and concurrency-safe in the engine, timeline states cover modification/cancel/sold-out/refund flows, Asia/Taipei fixed business-time evaluation is enforced, and hard pre-launch SLO gate artifacts and retained peak-load evidence (including order + pickup scenario and Grafana SLO dashboard) are present with passing status.

excludedApproaches: Best-effort inventory mutation without transactional guarantees.
Allowing post-cutoff modifications without hard denial.
Operating without hard performance gate definitions.
executionConstraints: Implement a strict order lifecycle with atomic reservation and release behavior.
Evaluate cutoff with fixed Asia/Taipei business-time rules.
Satisfy hard, pre-launch SLO gate for peak ordering throughput and latency.
Keep special-request handling in controlled schema boundaries.
name: Order State and Inventory Engine
rationale: Transaction correctness prevents count drift and downstream reconciliation errors.
summary: Implement robust order lifecycle and inventory integrity controls.
